mechanism_narrative: The Federal Reserve has raised FEDFUNDS to 2.56% as of September 2022 in a tightening cycle. Continued monetary restriction signals sustained upward pressure on real discount rates, which compresses present values of future cash flows and raises long-term Treasury yields as the market prices in persistent higher rates over the 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.56% as of September 2022 reflects active tightening cycle
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over the 20-day forward window despite FEDFUNDS remaining elevated, or FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% within the 20-day period without a corresponding yield decline.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 3.5% signals a tight labor market with persistent wage pressure. If UNRATE remains below 3.7% over the next 20 trading days, sustained labor cost inflation will force the Fed to maintain or increase rates, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward as market participants expect prolonged monetary tightness.
regime_hint: tight labor market with inflation concerns
failure_condition: UNRATE rises above 4.0% within 20 trading days and 10-year Treasury yield falls, or yield falls despite UNRATE staying below 3.7%.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 683,974 million dollars and housing starts (HOUST) at 1,575 thousand units indicate resilient consumer demand. If both series remain above their 3-month moving averages over the next 60 trading days, sustained demand growth will require the Fed to keep rates higher for longer, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market prices in extended monetary restriction.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both remain above their respective 3-month moving averages
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 683,974M and HOUST at 1,575K as of August-September 2022
failure_condition: Either RSAFS or HOUST falls below their 3-month moving average within 60 trading days, or 10-year Treasury yield declines despite both series remaining elevated.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 104.5464 (index 2017=100) shows supply-side capacity constraints. If INDPRO growth stalls or declines over the next 20 trading days, persistent supply shortages will sustain inflation expectations, forcing the Fed to signal higher-for-longer rates, which pushes 10-year Treasury yields upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month or grows less than 0.2%
failure_condition: INDPRO accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month growth within 20 trading days and 10-year Treasury yield falls, or yield rises despite INDPRO growth exceeding 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) at 296.761 reflects cumulative inflation of approximately 8.2% year-over-year as of September 2022. If CPI remains above 295 over the next 5 trading days, the market will price in expectations that the Fed must deliver additional rate hikes beyond current levels, causing 10-year Treasury yields to spike upward in anticipation of higher terminal rates.
regime_hint: high-inflation regime requiring aggressive monetary tightening
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL falls below 295.0 within 5 trading days and 10-year Treasury yield declines, or yield rises despite CPI falling below 295.0.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 153,018 thousand persons shows strong labor market momentum. If PAYEMS growth decelerates to below 200,000 new jobs per month over the next 60 trading days, the Fed will perceive reduced inflation pressure from labor costs and may signal a pause or pivot in rate hikes, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as market participants reduce expectations for higher terminal rates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth falls below 200,000 jobs
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth remains above 250,000 jobs per month for the entire 60-day period and 10-year Treasury yield rises, or yield falls despite PAYEMS growth exceeding 250,000.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 19,895.271 billion chained 2017 dollars and retail sales (RSAFS) at 683,974 million dollars show demand resilience. If both series decline simultaneously over the next 60 trading days, the market will interpret this as recession risk, causing a flight-to-quality bid in long-duration Treasuries that pushes 10-year yields downward as growth expectations collapse.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts quarter-over-quarter and RSAFS declines month-over-month for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,895.271B and RSAFS at 683,974M as of Q2 2022 and August 2022
failure_condition: GDPC1 and RSAFS both remain positive over the 60-day period and 10-year Treasury yield rises, or yield falls despite both series showing growth.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between elevated FEDFUNDS (2.56%) and high CPIAUCSL (296.761) signals that the Fed is in an aggressive tightening cycle with inflation still above target. If FEDFUNDS rises above 3.5% within the next 20 trading days while CPIAUCSL remains above 295, the market will price in a higher terminal rate, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise sharply as the real rate equilibrium shifts upward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 3.5% and CPIAUCSL remains above 295.0
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.56% and CPIAUCSL at 296.761 as of September 2022
regime_hint: aggressive monetary tightening with persistent inflation
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS remains below 3.5% for the entire 20-day period, or CPIAUCSL falls below 295.0, or 10-year Treasury yield declines despite both conditions being met.
